parts.answer: A description that contains five of the following:  pondweed set up with suitable apparatus to collect gas (1)  use of at least two filters of {different / named} colours or one (coloured) filter and white light (1)  sodium hydrogen carbonate added (in excess) (1)  give pondweed time to acclimatise (1)  volume of gas collected in stated time (1)  identification and contro l of one variable (1)  repeats and calculate means (to compare) (1) Accept diagram of apparatus Accept named wavelengths (instead of colours) eg in the range 480 – 750nm eg 3-30 minutes eg. temperature – eg use a heat shield pH – buffer length of pondweed – measure light intensity – bulb at fixed distance / wattage (5) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark

An explanation that includ es three of the following:  (grana formed from) stacks of membrane (1)  therefore providing large surface area (for photosystems / enzymes) (1)  containing {pigments / named pigments} to absorb light (1)  containing {enzymes / named enzymes} (for light dependent reactions) (1) Accept pigments releasing excited electrons Accept containing {electron carriers / electron transport chain} Accept enzymes providing {materials / named materials} for the light independent reactions. (3) (Total for Question 1 = 8 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes: 22.5 breaths per minute (1) Accept 21 / 24 breaths per minute (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer including the following:  estimation of tidal volume (1)  estimation of respirator y minute ventilation (1) Accept a value in the range 0.6 – 0.75 Accept a value in the range 13.2 – 18.0 (Allow ecf) Correct answer with no working gains full marks (2) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation including two of the following:  more oxygen is needed for increased (aerobic) respiration (1)  because {muscles / muscle cells} need more {ATP / energy} (for contraction) (1)  because more carbon dioxide ({is being produced by respiration / must be removed}) (1) Accept more oxygen needed for {conversion of lactic acid to pyruvate / oxidation of lactate} (2) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer including one abio tic and one biotic factor: Abiotic (1)  (room) temperature  humidity  altitude Biotic (1)  {age / sex} of participant  {health / fitness level} of participant  lung capacity  {intensity / duration} of exercise Accept oxygen concentration carbon dioxide concentration Accept BMI / smoking (2) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes the following:  variable with suitable control method described (1)  results are not valid / description of expected effect on the dependent variable (1) (2) (Total for Question 2

An answer that includes the following:  Suitable risk identified (1)  Suitable control identified (1) Any reasonable risk identified eg slips and trips, animal bites, harm from vegetation, allergies to plants, sunburn, danger from strangers, adverse weather Any corresponding control identified eg suitable footwear, insect repelle nt, suitable clothing, sunscreen, do not work alone etc (2) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes the following: There is no (significant) co rrelation between the mean length of marram grass and position on the dune (1) (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes the following:  axes labelled including units, and linear scale (1)  data plotted correctly in scatter graph format (1) Mean length (of leaves) /cm and position on dune /m Allow one plotting error (2) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes the following:  calculation of intermediate step (1)  calculation of r s (1) Example calculation Accept Σ d = 8 and n=7 OR Σ d = 0.143 / 0.14 n(n ‐ ) r s = 0.857 / 0.86 Correct answer with no working gains full marks (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that includes two of the following:  correct critical value stated (1)  stated calculated value is greater than the stated critical value, therefore reject the null hypothesis (1)  there is a (significant) correlation between position on dune and (mean) length of leaves (1) ie 0.786 Accept 0.857 > 0.786, so reject the null hypothesis for mp1 and 2 Accept stated calculated value is less than the stated critical value, therefore accept the null hypothesis Accept correct statement based on values given for bp2 Accept as the position on the dune increases the leaves are longer. Ignore any comment on variability of data (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that includes three of the following:  measure more plants at each position on dune (1)  measure plants at more heights on the dune (1)  use of measuring tape instead of ruler (as some plants are more than one metre long) (1)  suggestion of how to av oid bias when choosing plants or leaves to measure (1) Accept repeat investigation at a different place on dune ie another transect Accept repeat on different dunes Accept measure more than 10 plants (at each position on dune) Accept smaller intervals or to top of dune Accept eg random sampling / longest leaf on each plant / other relevant suggestion (3) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes:  a suitable factor (1) Accept pH / windspeed / soil moisture / so il humus content / salinity / mineral content / water availability (1) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark A description that includes the following:  use of correct {equipment / method} (1)  standardisation (1)  location of samples (1) Allow ecf from 3e(i) Accept use of probe / meter if appropriate. Accept eg collection of soil sample, weighi ng, heating and reweighing eg sample {at known depth / meter at known height above dune surface} / sample for same time / sample at same time eg sample at {every / each} position on the dune / sample every 2 metres along transect (3) (Total for question 3 = 16 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that includes three of the following:  find a suitable {concentration / volume} of pineapple juice (1)  find a suitable timescale for growth of bacteria (1)  find a suitable {temperature / pH} for {growth of bacteria / incubation / activity of enzyme} (1)  find a suitable way to count bacteria (1) Accept find a suitable range of concentrations Accept find a suitable method of making the pineapple juice Accept find storage time (of pineapple juice) which has an effect Accept identify suitable growth medium Accept find a suitable method for comparing growth (rate) (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that includes eight of the following:  clear statement of the dependent variable (1)  method of producing pineapple juice (1)  (use of nutrient broth) with and without pineapple juice (1)  use of aseptic technique (1)  samples taken at suitable intervals (1)  suitable method for counting bacteria (1)  method of calculation of growth rate (1)  identify one variable to be controlled and description of how it is controlled (1)  identify second variable to be controlled and description of how it is controlled (1)  repeats for each set up or repeat the whole investigation (1) Accept {number of bacteria / turbidity / absorbance} with ref to time Accept heated juice / water / sugar solution as a control Accept several concentrations of juice as long one is zero. Accept comparison of fresh and stored eg flaming neck of bottles, work near Bunsen burner etc Accept bacteria counted at start and after suitable time Accept {absorbance / transmission} with {colorimeter / datalogger} Accept serial dilution and plating Accept : temperature, pH, {age / variety / part / tissue} of pineapple, {concentration / volume} of juice, {age / volume / concentrat ion} of bacterial culture {composition / concentration / volume} of growth medium (8) Question Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that includes the following:  raw data table with headings and appropriate units, with means calculated from repeats (1)  graph with both labelled axes (1)  use of an appropriate statistical test (1) Accept description of calculating mean (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that includes three of the following:  {clumping / uneven distribution} of bacteria makes it difficult to count (1)  factors related to colorimeter use (1)  relevant named factor may affect growth (rate) of bacteria (1)  second relevant named factor may affect growth (rate) of bacteria (1) Accept hard to add the same number of bacteria to each culture at the start Accept factors related to serial dilution and plating (1) Ignore pH and temperature (3) (Total for question 4 = 17 marks) Pearson Education Limited.
